payload.title: RT Comprehensive rename: Rustricted → Trust
payload.description:
Total rename of the project from "Rustricted" to "Trust" across every artifact in the workspace. No references to "Rustricted" or "rustricted" should remain anywhere in committed code, docs, fixtures, case studies, examples, CI, scope state, or memory files. The lowercase crate prefix changes from rustricted-* to trust-*.

## Naming decisions

- Workspace + crate names: rustricted-* → trust-*; main CLI: rustricted → trust
- Bins: trust, trust-rustc, trust-rustdoc, trust-lsp, cargo-trust
- Proc-macro path: rustricted_attrs::strict!{} → trust_attrs::strict!{} (macro NAME `strict!` is preserved; it still means "strict mode on")
- Lint namespace in attrs: #[allow(rustricted::R0014, reason = "...")] → #[allow(trust::R0014, reason = "...")]
- Env vars: every RUSTRICTED_* → TRUST_*
- Cache dir: $TMPDIR/rustricted-cache/ → $TMPDIR/trust-cache/
- Subcommand: cargo rustricted → cargo trust
- Scope project: rename to "Trust" (key stays RT for existing-ticket continuity)

## What stays the same

- Lint rule codes (R0001..R0016, R0042, R3001) — they're identifiers, not project name
- Macro name `strict!` — semantically still right
- Example fixture dir names (cargo-strict-fixture-*, examples/01-lints/strict-clean.rs, etc.) — they describe strict MODE, not the project
- src/strict.rs module name in trust-lints
- Branch name audit/effects — historical

## Files / artifacts that must be touched

- Cargo.toml (workspace root)
- Every crates/*/Cargo.toml (package name, [bin]/[lib] name, path deps)
- Every crates/*/src/**/*.rs (use stmts, string literals, comments, doc strings, env-var lookups, cache-dir consts, lint-namespace parser in trust-lints/src/allow.rs)
- examples/** (every rustricted_attrs::strict!{} invocation, comments, READMEs)
- case-studies/heck-strict.md, case-studies/tre-strict.md, case-studies/dogfooding.md
- case-studies/heck-strict/**, case-studies/tre-strict/** (vendored sources have strict markers using rustricted_attrs)
- docs/SPEC.md, docs/AGENTS.md, docs/WHY.md
- README.md
- scripts/fmt.sh (skip-list comments, log banners)
- .github/workflows/ci.yml (step names, log strings, paths)
- eval/tasks.toml (the rustricted_prompt text mentions the language by name)
- xtask gen-docs / gen-std-signatures string literals
- .gitignore (if "rustricted" appears)
- ~/.claude/projects/-Users-bri-dev-One/memory/MEMORY.md
- ~/.claude/projects/-Users-bri-dev-One/memory/project_rustricted.md (rename file to project_trust.md too)
- ~/.claude/projects/-Users-bri-dev-One/memory/feedback_rustricted_design.md (rename file to feedback_trust_design.md)

## Crate directory renames (use `git mv` to preserve history)

crates/rustricted/         → crates/trust/
crates/rustricted-attrs/   → crates/trust-attrs/
crates/rustricted-diag/    → crates/trust-diag/
crates/rustricted-lints/   → crates/trust-lints/
crates/rustricted-lower/   → crates/trust-lower/
crates/rustricted-lsp/     → crates/trust-lsp/
crates/rustricted-rustc/   → crates/trust-rustc/
crates/rustricted-rustdoc/ → crates/trust-rustdoc/
crates/rustricted-std/     → crates/trust-std/
crates/rustricted-syntax/  → crates/trust-syntax/
cargo-rustricted/          → cargo-trust/ (if it exists at workspace root)

## Acceptance

1. `grep -ri 'rustricted' --exclude-dir=target --exclude-dir=.git .` returns ZERO hits inside the repo
2. `grep -ri 'Rustricted' --exclude-dir=target --exclude-dir=.git .` returns ZERO hits
3. `cargo build --workspace` works (under RUSTC_WRAPPER=$(realpath target/debug/trust-rustc))
4. `cargo test --workspace` — 133+ tests still pass
5. All examples in examples/ build and produce expected output (00-hello, 01-lints, 02-pipe, 03-named-args, cargo-strict-fixture, cargo-strict-fixture-multimod, cargo-strict-fixture-cross-file, cargo-strict-cross-crate)
6. Both case studies (heck-strict, tre-strict) still build under the new wrapper
7. CI workflow updated; no leftover references
8. README, WHY, SPEC, AGENTS rewritten with the new name
9. Memory files updated; index reflects new file names
10. Git history preserved via `git mv` for directory renames
11. Single clean commit (or small handful) with a clear, comprehensive message
